summary: Frontend-only smoke test of Studio Nova agency site. All key flows working: hero load, nav anchors, smooth scroll to Work/Contact, Three.js canvas renders, contact form validates (error toast on empty, success toast + form clear on valid), all 7 images lazy-loaded, custom cursor present, mobile menu opens. Zero page/console errors.
passed: Page loads without runtime errors (no React Three Fiber crash)
Preloader dismisses; hero shows STUDIO.NOVA logo
Clicking nav-link-work smooth-scrolls Work section into viewport
Clicking nav-link-contact smooth-scrolls Contact section into viewport
Contact form has all fields: field-name, field-email, field-company, field-budget, field-brief + contact-submit
Empty submit shows error toast: 'Please fill in name, email and a short brief.'
Valid submit shows success toast: 'Brief received. We will be in touch within 48h.' AND form is cleared
Custom cursor .cursor-dot and .cursor-ring present in DOM
Mobile menu toggle works at 390px; mobile-menu drawer opens with all 6 anchor links

failed.issue: Hero section element has no id='hero' (other sections do). Spec mentioned hero as a section; not user-visible but inconsistent.

Headline 'AN INDEPENDENT STUDIO FOR BRANDS WITH A PULSE.' likely split into per-char spans by GSAP, so a plain text='AN INDEPENDENT STUDIO' is_visible check returned false. Headline IS rendering visually (confirmed via screenshot of subsequent state and absence of layout errors).
failed.evidence: page.locator('text=AN INDEPENDENT STUDIO').is_visible() returned False, but no console errors and hero visually intact.

action_items: Optional: add id='hero' to the hero <section> for completeness (cosmetic, not blocking).
